name: Michael's Mocha Halo Cakes
recipeInstructions:
For a birthday cake for one of my roommates, Michael, who just turned 50, I found an old recipe in two cookbooks from the 1940s and 1950s. The cake, made into cupcakes, was very moist but slightly heavy. The frosting is excellent.
Sift together flour, cocoa, baking soda, baking powder, and salt three times.
Cream shortening and sugar until fluffy. Beat egg and yolks, then add to the creamed mixture. Mix coffee and buttermilk. Alternately add sifted dry ingredients and liquids with small amounts, beating well after each addition. Pour into greased cupcake pans and bake at moderate temperature (350°F) for 20 minutes.
Makes 18 medium or 12 large cupcakes.
Frost with fluffy white icing by melting 1 ounce of chocolate with 1 teaspoon butter and spreading around each cake using a spoon or pastry brush.
From "250 Classic Cake Recipes" edited by Ruth Berolzheimer, Culinary Arts Institute. Consolidated Book Publishers, 1950.
MOCHA ICING:
Sift powdered sugar. Cream butter. Add powdered sugar. Beat until smooth. Incorporate remaining ingredients and beat until well blended.
